zh.Stapleton, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾斯塔普萊頓

zh-hans.Stapleton, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾斯塔普萊頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clare于1215年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾斯塔普萊頓。他于大约1243年与Nichole de Camville在英国格盧斯特斯爾斯塔普萊頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Dev .

variants.name: Deborah
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘bee’ in Hebrew), borne by the nurse of Rebecca (Genesis 35:8) and by a woman judge and prophet (Judges 4–5) who led the Israelites to victory over the Canaanites. It has always been popular as a Jewish name. It was in use among Christians by the mid 16th century and was taken up by the Puritans in the 17th century, in part because the bee was a symbol of industriousness. Since then it has enjoyed enormous popularity, peaking in the 1960s. Among other famous bearers is the actress  Deborah Kerr  (  1921–2007 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clare was born in 1215, in Stapleton, Gloucestershire, England. He married Nichole de Camville about 1243, in Stapleton, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clare est né en 1215, à Stapleton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Nichole de Camville environ 1243, à Stapleton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clare wurde 1215 in Stapleton, Gloucestershire, England geboren. Er hat um 1243 in Stapleton, Gloucestershire, England Nichole de Camville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clare è nato il nel 1215 nel Stapleton, Gloucestershire, England. Lui sposato Nichole de Camville circa 1243, nel Stapleton, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Stapleton, Gloucestershire, England: イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスタプレトン
ja.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clareは1215年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスタプレトンで生まれた。約1243年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスタプレトンでNichole de Camvilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clare(은)는 1215년 영국 그루스스터실르 스타플레턴에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1243년 영국 그루스스터실르 스타플레턴에서 Nichole de Camville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clare nasceu em 1215, em Stapleton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Nichole de Camville aproximadamente 1243, em Stapleton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clare родился в 1215 г. в местности стаплетон, глоусестиршир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Nichole de Camville около 1243 г. в местности стаплетон, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: John de Saint Clare nació en 1215, en Stapleton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Nichole de Camville aproximadamente 1243, en Stapleton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
